required_edits: Fix the required-vs-required test asymmetry for git.mjs sources: git.mjs's committed closure is {clotho/registry.mjs, clotho/weavers/git.mjs} and it does NOT import util.mjs, yet the task's mandate 'both weavers ... consume only counted sources' plus D29 requires the weaver to exhaust EVERY handed iterable; git.mjs iterates sources['package-symbols'] then sources['package-files'] — acceptable — but there is no unit proving the weaver exhausts BOTH iterables when one is empty-but-present or proving behavior when a handed iterable is left unconsumed (the driver-side fatal accounting check is Task 5, but Task 4a's own text requires units proving exhaustion semantics against the weaver; test-git.mjs only asserts the happy-path exhausted:true). Add a unit where one counted source is empty and both accountings still read exhausted:true after weave().

scanExports mis-classifies `export const x = 1` when phase1 regex is applied: the phase1 regex `export\s+(?:async\s+function|function|const\s+[A-Za-z_$][A-Za-z0-9_$]*\s*=?|class)\s*` and declRe overlap but phase1 permits `const NAME` with OPTIONAL `=`, meaning `export const x;` (illegal JS but lexically matchable) or `export const x = require(...)` dynamic flow is silently accepted as a Phase 1 export rather than warned as dynamic symbol flow. The frozen grammar is exactly 'export function|async function|const|class followed by an identifier'; `export const x = <arbitrary dynamic expression>` IS within the frozen grammar, so this is arguably faithful — but add a unit pinning `export const x = someCall()` as a seeded export so the boundary is committed behavior, not accident.

test-closure.mjs section 1 asserts committed inventories equal derived closures, but WEAVER_IMPL_FILES['clotho-git-weaver'] omits clotho/weavers/util.mjs while git.mjs is claimed (in its header comment) to have closure exactly {registry.mjs, git.mjs} — verify and pin with an explicit unit that git.mjs contains NO import of util.mjs (e.g. assert classifyModuleLoads over git.mjs source yields only '../registry.mjs'), because a future drive-by import of util.mjs into git.mjs would be caught by closure equality only if the committed inventory is not simultaneously 'fixed' in the same commit; a redundant explicit assertion documents reviewed intent.

code.mjs treats r.kind === 'ambiguous-extension' silently for non-.mjs RELATIVE imports (e.g. './helper.json' emits nothing). The task text says code.mjs 'accepts static relative imports with an explicit .mjs target' and 'unrepresentable-consumer only for imports whose specifier does not resolve to a real file below the closed roots'. A relative './helper.json' import that resolves to a real seeded file is neither accepted (.mjs grammar) nor unresolvable — silence is a defensible reading, but the fixture list in the task requires 'an import specifier resolves to no file' to warn; ADD a unit pinning that a relative non-.mjs specifier to a MISSING file (e.g. './missing.json') is also silent (currently ambiguous-extension short-circuits before existence is checked) so the precedence is committed, reviewed behavior.

physicalContainment accepts a nonexistent tail under a real chain (returns true) — consistent with D21's 'deepest existing ancestor' language — but walkFiles' componentsSymlinkFree returns {ok:true, missing:true} when ANY component mid-chain is missing, then 'continue's (absent root: nothing to walk). A missing INTERMEDIATE component with an existing deeper path cannot occur on real filesystems, but a TOCTOU rename between lstat calls can produce it; this is acceptable for Phase 1 yet undocumented. Document the race posture in a comment or normalize both helpers to one shared chain-walk primitive (containmentReal vs componentsSymlinkFree vs physicalContainment currently implement three near-identical walks — consolidation reduces drift risk for the D21/D33 shared-mechanics claim).
hard_stops: Task text requires PACKAGE_ROOTS to inventory 'every current package root' and the gate only proves the file exists — but the committed list includes 'connectors/ai-peer-mcp' while the imports-permitted gate check and spine-read-only constraint enumerate merkle-dag/build-gate/breakout as spine; nothing in the provided artifacts proves 'connectors/ai-peer-mcp' is a real package root at this PR (test-inventory.mjs statSync would fail if absent, and the gate says tests pass, so existence is proven) — HOWEVER the completeness direction (no package root OMITTED) is proven nowhere: no test walks the repository for package.json files and asserts the committed list covers them. The task freezes 'inventory every current package root once and commit the exact sorted paths'; without a completeness check this is an unverifiable hand claim. Require either a unit enumerating repo-top-level package manifests and asserting set-equality with PACKAGE_ROOTS, or an explicit human-reviewed attestation in the PR that the five roots are exhaustive at head 8dae45ea.

The counted-iterator final-item semantics (test-util section 8) codify observed_count = N-1 when the consumer breaks after processing the last item: 'a source that raises before completion is not counted' is satisfied, but D26 defines 'inspected' as opened+read+processed to edge-extraction eligibility WITHOUT fatal error — a consumer that fully processed item N and then breaks has inspected N items, yet the driver-held accessor reads N-1 with exhausted:false, which under D29 would be fatal accounting failure for a weaver that legitimately processed everything but exited its loop early. The implementation makes run-to-natural-completion the ONLY way to get counted(N)+exhausted, which is a STRICTER contract than D26's definition of inspected. This strictness is defensible (it forces for...of to completion) and the test documents it, but it means the count is NOT 'incremented when the consumer completes consumption of that source' as the task text states — it increments one iteration LATER. Since both weavers use for...of to completion this cannot misfire at this PR, and the driver check is Task 5; I flag it as a semantic divergence from the frozen text that must be either (a) ratified as the intended reading in Task 5's driver, or (b) fixed now by counting on the generator's resumption OR return()/throw() hooks. Because the divergence is observable only through the driver-held accessor and no driver exists at this PR, I do not treat it as an outright reject, but it must not silently propagate into Task 5.
rationale: The implementation is high quality and most of the frozen surface is faithfully implemented: the REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS table matches the normative table exactly with a deepEqual unit; LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS matches D32 exactly (keys {module,node:module}, values the sorted pair) with deep-equal, deep-freeze, exact-key/value, and mutation-attempt units; per D17/AM-17 only git+code inventories are committed and test-inventory proves every committed path exists; test-closure derives closures with the SHARED classifier/resolver from util.mjs and proves committed==derived (never trusted), covering all seven accepted forms via form-only fixtures, recursion/cycles/conditional-dynamic-import, permitted merkle-dag traversal, and the full fatal matrix (missing/extra/nonexistent inventory entries; unresolved/ambiguous/symlink/non-regular/escape/forbidden targets; symlinked/out-of-scope/nonexistent entries; non-literal and comment/string lookalikes); AM-34 test 19 is implemented with a genuinely thoughtful uniqueness sweep (one classifier, one resolver, no second .mjs-appending resolver anywhere in clotho/). git.mjs emits exactly the two frozen log shapes with strict full-SHA line validation (lone \n and internal blank lines fatal; empty string = warn/no-edge), first-result selection, git:<sha> source_ref, and the allowlist rejects reordered/duplicated/missing/extra flags. code.mjs produces all four endpoint shapes with symbol-vs-file threading, preserves no-export named imports as repository-file->code-symbol, dedups, ignores unused/comment/string uses, restricts unrepresentable-consumer to genuinely unresolvable specifiers, and fails closed on symlink/escape physical violations. Zero deps, ESM, node:+relative imports only, diff confined to clotho/. However I cannot sign approve AS-IS on two grounds detailed in hard_stops: (1) PACKAGE_ROOTS completeness ('every current package root') is asserted by no test and not attested in the provided evidence — the closed-inventory discipline (D8) makes omission the dangerous direction, and nothing here would catch a missed root; (2) the counted-iterator increments on the NEXT resumption rather than on completed consumption of the item itself, so a consumer that fully processed the final item but breaks records N-1/not-exhausted — a semantic divergence from the task's own words ('increments its private count only when the consumer completes consumption of that source') that is invisible at this PR but load-bearing for Task 5's fatal accounting checks; it needs either ratification or a fix before it propagates. The required_edits are smaller faithfulness pins (empty-source exhaustion unit for git.mjs, boundary pins for the export scanner and non-.mjs relative-import precedence, a redundancy assertion documenting git.mjs's util-free closure, and consolidation/documentation of the three near-identical chain-walkers). None of these are scope expansion; they pin frozen-text semantics that are currently accidental rather than proven.
